title: City Pilot Cuts Permit Review Time

chunks.text: Riverton's planning department finished a six-month pilot that moved permit review notes into a shared source ledger. The department says average review time fell from 23 days to 16 days across 412 residential permits. Staff attributed most of the gain to fewer duplicate document requests.

The pilot did not remove human review. Senior planner Mara Singh said every permit still received a final staff check before approval. The city delayed commercial permits until it can clarify who owns corrections when outside engineers upload conflicting drawings.

A neighborhood coalition supported faster turnaround but asked the city to publish monthly error reports. The council will vote on a permanent rollout in June after reviewing costs, appeal data, and public comments.

AtlasDB 2.4 adds incremental snapshot compaction for clusters with more than 500 GB of retained history. In internal testing, compaction reduced cold storage use by 38% with no measured increase in restore time.
Breaking change: the deprecated --unsafe-replay flag has been removed. Operators should replace it with --replay-mode=isolated and run atlas doctor before starting a replay job.
Known issue: compaction can stall on ARM workers when a shard has more than 2 million tiny records. The workaround is to set ATLAS_COMPACTION_BATCH=25000 until patch 2.4.1 ships.

title: Why does my side panel go blank after login?

chunks.text: Original poster: After login, the extension side panel opens but stays blank. Reloading the page fixes it for a few minutes. Console shows 'Cannot access chrome:// URL' even though the user is on a normal HTTPS page.

Maintainer reply: The blank panel is usually caused by the app trying to reinject a content script after Chrome rotates the frame ID. Version 1.8.2 adds a request ID handshake and retries the host injection once.
Accepted answer: Update to 1.8.2, then disable the old beta build. Two users confirmed the fix. One user still had trouble on internal company pages where extensions are blocked by policy.

title: Figuring out eyepieces - Page 3

surfaceInfo.label: Discussion
surfaceInfo.reason: forum-style URL path; repeated post blocks

chunks.text: Post #41: I am jumping in late, but the 8mm eyepiece advice assumes the original poster is using an f/5 Dobsonian. If the telescope is the f/10 SCT mentioned later, the magnification math changes.

Reply with 12 likes: For a first upgrade, do not buy a whole kit. Get one mid-power eyepiece around 15mm and one low-power finder eyepiece. Several members agreed that the kits include focal lengths beginners rarely use.
Moderator note: The first page has the original budget and telescope model. This page only has follow-up recommendations and corrections. Please quote the scope model when giving new advice.

forbidden: consensus is to buy a whole kit

title: Study Finds Mixed Results for Four-Day School Week

chunks.text: A state education office reviewed 74 districts that adopted a four-day school week. Rural districts reported teacher retention improving by 9 percentage points over three years, mostly in hard-to-staff middle school roles.

The same review found no statewide math improvement. In lower-income districts, fifth-grade math scores fell by 4 percentile points unless schools added tutoring or extended the remaining school days.
Parents were split. Survey responses praised fewer commutes and easier medical appointments, but 41% of hourly workers said childcare became harder on the fifth weekday.

La ciudad emitio un aviso de hervir agua para los barrios Norte y Alameda despues de una perdida de presion en la planta Este. El aviso comenzo a las 7:30 p.m. del martes.
Los residentes deben hervir el agua durante un minuto antes de beberla, cocinar o cepillarse los dientes. El agua embotellada esta disponible en la biblioteca central hasta las 9 p.m.
El departamento espera resultados de laboratorio el jueves por la manana. El aviso seguira activo hasta que dos pruebas consecutivas confirmen que el agua es segura.
